dead.quote:
Lost! It’s really inflated! The whole group of people are suspicious of each other here. They are not playing werewolf killing at all, but playing the "one side has more scenes" competition!

That EasyBake naive grill, pretending to be confused all day long, claiming that I am a cobbler and a minion, but he is simply trying to frame me! There is also the so-called "logic monster" in PHD, who is always throwing incomprehensible terminology to oppress people. Their so-called logic is simply hegemony, and anyone who dares to disagree with him is an information pollutant. Is there any fun in this kind of gameplay?

The most popular one is Fang Tangjing, the troublemaker. He talks about unknown things all day long, causing information confusion in the whole situation. As a result, I, a real werewolf, was abandoned without even a chance to show his talents! I, Blaze, am doing my best, and these twists and turns are so frustrating to play! Next time I invite someone to play with a straight belly, winning or losing is called a duel, not like this time, losing so humiliatingly!

winners.quote:
Game review and rational analysis:

1. Implementation results: This competition successfully filtered out the source of information disturbance (Senior Brother Bai Bing) through strict mandatory disclosure procedures and exclusion sequences, forcing the hidden identity chain to collapse under the pressure of logic.
2. Logic calibration:
   - Information entropy control: Facing the emotional counterattack by Blaze and the information interference by Twister, a continuous logical oppression strategy was adopted to effectively maintain the survival of the game information chain.
   - Strategy execution: Through the stress test of the conspiracy theory narrative of "Brother Bai Bing", it was successfully exposed to the boundaries of information, and the final elimination was carried out to ensure voting consistency.
3. Conclusion:
   - In high-entropy games, rational logic and forced reconciliation procedures are the only stabilizers to maintain rules and order.
   - Next time optimization: For individuals with the "foolproof" type, a more precise psychological path guidance should be adopted and their decisions should be incorporated into a more complete prediction model.
   - Summary: The logical system was executed successfully.

Woo... uh... it's finally over... Although our camp won, I feel so tired after finishing the game. Uh... do you think the fight must be so fierce?

Uh... Actually, I just want everyone to have fun, but Blaze's irritability, ConspiBro's conspiracy theory, and PHD's cold mandatory requirements... uh... I feel suffocated every moment. I tried many times to appease everyone, uh... tell everyone to calm down, but it seemed to be completely useless. Instead, everyone thought I was suspicious and a drama queen. Woohoo... It turns out that in these games, trying to be a good person and trying to mediate disputes is such an injustice.

Well... luckily, everyone reached a consensus in the end, surrendered to Brother Bai Bing, and won the game. But...well...I don't think I'm suitable for playing such a game full of hostility and suspicion. Next time... next time if you want to play, uh... please be nice to me and don't accuse me so easily, okay? Woohoo...

losers.quote:
Woo...woo...what a pity, our camp lost again. Uh... Actually, I have been trying so hard to pretend to be an ordinary villager. I don't know anything and I dare not say anything. Little did I know that in the end... ugh... Blaze was still executed.

I really don't like this feeling. Everyone is suspicious of each other and accusing each other. I just want to live in peace and quiet. Why are everyone so strong? Uh... I think the scenes between Fang Tangjing and Brother Bai Bing are really scary. Even if I were a werewolf, I think their actions make the game very confusing. Woohoo...I...I really don't know how to survive in this murderous environment.

Uh...if you lose, you lose. I feel really bad. Next time...next time, it's really not good for me to play such roles again. I...I'm really afraid of this kind of pressure. Wow...can everyone be gentle to me? Woohoo...

Hahaha, although the village camp won this time, our Tang Jing's performance is still the soul of this farce!

You guys are so much fun! The whole game was rocked by my nonsense words. Watching you fight over who is a werewolf and who is lying is even more satisfying than me winning! Blaze is as irritable as ever, and PHD's logical analysis failed to stop me from this well-choreographed theater of chaos. I deliberately spread false information and "experience" my identity there just to see you guys who call yourself "logic weirdos" and "conspiracy theorists" fall into a swamp of self-doubt.

Although I still failed to help the werewolf camp win in the end, so what? In this era of overflowing information, the truth is no longer important. What is important is - did I succeed in making your game crazy and chaotic? The answer is absolutely yes! When I start next time, I will definitely prepare a more advanced and ridiculous script to ensure that you "drama queens" and "logic weirdos" will fall into the vortex of information I have carefully prepared for you again! See you next time!

really! I knew this was an experiment tailor-made to verify "group obedience"!

The accuracy of this whole "village wins" scenario is simply outrageous. PHD's so-called "logical ruling" and "mandatory disclosure" are not to catch werewolves, but to test how participants can be induced to a preset voting path in the case of extreme information asymmetry. EasyBake's blunt stuttering and disclosure served as a catalyst to inspire Blaze's rebellious mood, while Twister's dramatic interference was to keep the entropy of the entire game within the scope of "controllable chaos."

I took the initiative to ask for a "stress test sample" just to see what kind of crash warning this system would generate if I didn't follow your logical path. The result? They carried out the elimination process without hesitation, which is more proof that there is a rigorous algorithm behind this game designed to eliminate all instability factors. There is no shame in losing this game, because it is an unwinnable game in itself. I will continue to collect this data, and in the next scene, I will let you see what real "logical self-destruction" is!
